summary: Phase 9.A.2 (title-screen UI per docs/PHASES.md:582 deliverable + acceptance criterion 1) approved. Phase 9 — the FINAL phase before v1 release — is decomposed into 9.A.1 → 9.A.8 sandbox-resolvable sub-phases plus a 9.B live-deploy verification per the previous 9.A.1 approval's split rationale. Phase 9.A.2 is the load-bearing chunk that satisfies acceptance criterion 1 ('A first-time visitor can land on the GitHub Pages URL, click "New Run," and play to floor 1 without reading docs'). Concrete deliverables: (1) src/ui/title-screen.ts — new pure rendering module 'renderTitleScreen(host, options)' as a peer of src/ui/win-screen.ts (Phase 7) and src/ui/{hud,inventory,equipment}.ts. The module is read-only (Phase 5 frozen contract: no Date access, no PRNG, no Math.random — the date is passed in via options.todayDate from the caller). Idempotent re-render preserves the user-typed seed input value across subsequent calls; only the date-derived 'Random Seed (today: YYYY-MM-DD)' button label updates across midnight UTC. Builds: heading 'ICEFALL' + subtitle + seed input field (pre-filled with today's date) + 3 buttons (New Run / Random Seed / Paste Fingerprint) + hidden paste-textarea row revealed by the Paste button + footer help text ('Tab to navigate · Enter to activate'). Wires keyboard handlers: Enter on the seed input activates New Run; click handlers on each button. (2) src/main.ts — new shouldShowTitleScreen() predicate + todayUtcDate() helper + window flag __TITLE_SCREEN__: 'active' | 'skipped' | undefined. The bootstrap() flow now gates on shouldShowTitleScreen(): on the bare URL (no ?seed=, no ?run=, no #seed= anywhere), the title screen renders ABOVE the diagnostic surface and the playable game is skipped; on any seed source present, the title screen is skipped and startGame runs. Title-screen click handlers: onNewRun + onRandomSeed navigate via window.location.assign(`?seed=<s>`) → full page reload → bootstrap re-enters with a seed source → game boots. onPasteFingerprint navigates to a pasted URL (HTTP/HTTPS scheme allowlist per code-review S1 fix) or constructs `?run=<fp>` for raw fingerprints. (3) src/main.ts startGame seed source — preferentially reads `?seed=` (Phase 8 query convention) over `#seed=` (Phase 5 hash-fragment legacy); both paths preserved for backward-compat. (4) style.css — ~100 lines of title-screen styling at the tail (border + button rows + primary-action accent color matching the cyberpunk neon palette + paste-row dashed border + footer help-text muted style). Inherits the prefers-reduced-motion gate + :focus-visible outline from 9.A.1. (5) tests/ui/fake-dom.ts extended with addEventListener + dispatch + style + focus + typed properties (value, placeholder, rows, tabIndex, etc.) — the existing fake DOM was minimal and didn't support title-screen's interactive surface. The test-only `dispatch(event, eventArg)` helper fires registered handlers without a real DOM. (6) tests/ui/title-screen.test.ts — 16 unit tests covering DOM structure (heading/subtitle/seed-input/3 buttons/paste row/footer), pre-fill behavior, host classList, primary-action styling, click handlers (New Run + trim() + empty-skip + Enter-activates + ignore-non-Enter + Random Seed + Paste submit + empty-paste-skip), and idempotent re-render (no DOM duplication + date label updates across midnight UTC + user-typed seed preserved across re-renders). (7) tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts — 9 net-new e2e tests (Phase 9 title screen renders / pre-fills today / SKIPPED on ?seed= / New Run navigates to ?seed=<s> / Random Seed navigates to ?seed=<today> / Paste textarea reveal / Enter on seed input / diagnostic page still renders / + S2 regression: empty ?seed= still shows title screen). 5 existing game-flag tests updated from page.goto('/') to page.goto('/?seed=diagnostic-sample') to bypass the new title-screen gate. (8) Code-review fixes folded in: S1 (security — onPasteFingerprint now allowlists http(s): only, rejecting javascript: / data: URIs that would be a self-XSS vector); S2 (UX — shouldShowTitleScreen requires non-empty ?seed=, ?run=, #seed= values; an empty `?seed=` no longer silently routes to the hardcoded 'diagnostic-sample' fallback). All sandbox gates green: 1170 tests passing across 82 test files (was 1154/81 in 9.A.1; +16 unit tests / +1 file from title-screen.test.ts; +9 e2e tests added but those are Playwright-only, not in vitest count); 0 lint errors; 0 typecheck errors; build dist/assets/index-*.js = 143.85 KB raw / 42.49 KB gzipped (was 139.20/41.30 in 9.A.1 = +4.65 KB raw / +1.19 KB gzipped); CSS 5.61 KB raw / 1.60 KB gzipped. Well under the 110 KB CI gate. Cross-runtime golden chain preserved unchanged (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST). Phase 5 frozen contract preserved (src/ui/title-screen.ts uses no Date / Math.random / performance.now / floats — the date is sourced from main.ts which IS allowed to use Date).
suggested_commit_message: phase-9.A.2: title-screen UI per docs/PHASES.md:582 + acceptance criterion 1 (renderTitleScreen module + main.ts gating + 16 unit tests + 9 e2e tests + S1 self-XSS fix + S2 empty-?seed= regression fix)

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm that Phase 9.A.2's title-screen UI satisfies acceptance criterion 1 of docs/PHASES.md:589 ('A first-time visitor can land on the GitHub Pages URL, click "New Run," and play to floor 1 without reading docs') without breaking any prior-phase invariant. The title screen renders BEFORE the playable game on the bare URL; clicking 'New Run' navigates to `?seed=<s>` and the page reloads into the game. The Phase 5 frozen contract on src/ui/** (no Date access) is preserved by passing the date in as an option from main.ts. The Phase 8 share-URL flow is preserved (URLs with `?run=` or `?seed=` skip the title screen). The cross-runtime golden chain is unchanged.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 1170 passed across 82 test files (was 1154/81 in Phase 9.A.1). Net change: +16 unit tests / +1 file from tests/ui/title-screen.test.ts. The cross-runtime golden chain (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST) is preserved verbatim. The 16 new tests cover DOM structure, click/keyboard handlers, idempotent re-render, and edge cases (empty seed skips New Run; trim() applied; Enter-only triggers; non-Enter keys ignored).
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. The new src/ui/title-screen.ts conforms to the Phase 5 frozen contract (verified by the existing src/ui/** lint scope: no Date global, no Math.random, no performance.now, no floats). The main.ts changes (shouldShowTitleScreen + todayUtcDate + bootstrap branch + onPasteFingerprint scheme allowlist) pass the existing main-scope lint rules (Date IS allowed in main.ts since it's the orchestrator).
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors. The new TitleScreenOptions type is consumed cleanly; the test file uses a small `q(host, selector)` + `makeHost()` helper to bridge between the WHATWG Element type signature and the FakeElement runtime type without introducing per-call casts.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green. dist/assets/index-*.js = 143.85 KB raw / 42.49 KB gzipped (was 139.20/41.30 in Phase 9.A.1 = +4.65 KB raw / +1.19 KB gzipped from the title-screen module + main.ts wiring). dist/assets/index-*.css = 5.61 KB raw / 1.60 KB gzipped (was 4.26/1.36 = +1.35 KB raw / +0.24 KB gzipped from the ~100 lines of title-screen styling). Bundle remains well under the 110 KB CI gate (+~67 KB headroom).
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: User-visible behavior change in 9.A.2: the bare URL (https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/) now renders the title screen ABOVE the diagnostic surface, with the playable game suppressed until a seed is chosen. URLs with `?seed=`, `?run=`, or `#seed=` skip the title screen and boot directly into the game (preserves the Phase 5 deep-link UX and the Phase 8 share-URL flow). The 5 existing game-flag e2e tests were updated to navigate with `?seed=diagnostic-sample` to bypass the new gate. Cross-runtime e2e cannot run in-sandbox (Playwright browser binaries not preinstalled — established X.B sandbox limitation per docs/PHASES.md and the deploy.yml `npx playwright install --with-deps` step on the ubuntu-24.04 runner); the new __TITLE_SCREEN__ window flag will be exercised on the deploy.yml CI run that lands this commit.

title_screen_module_pure.evidence: src/ui/title-screen.ts implements renderTitleScreen(host, options): void per the Phase 5 frozen contract — read-only sink (no mutation of imported state), no Date access in src/ui/** (the date is passed in via options.todayDate from main.ts which IS allowed to use Date), no PRNG, no Math.random. Idempotent re-render: the function rebuilds the DOM skeleton on first call and updates only the date-derived button label on subsequent calls; the user-typed seed input value is preserved. The 16 unit tests in tests/ui/title-screen.test.ts cover DOM structure (8 tests), click/keyboard handlers (8 tests), idempotent re-render (3 tests).

title_screen_gates_correctly.evidence: src/main.ts shouldShowTitleScreen() returns true on the bare URL (no seed source) and false when any of `?seed=`, `?run=`, or `#seed=` has a NON-EMPTY value. Per code-review S2 fix: empty `?seed=` (no value) does NOT count as a seed source; same for empty `?run=` and `#seed=`. The downstream startGame consumer requires length > 0 on the seed; without the S2 fix, an empty `?seed=` would silently route to the hardcoded 'diagnostic-sample' fallback. The bootstrap() function gates on shouldShowTitleScreen() and either renders the title screen + diagnostic surface (no game) OR runs startGame + diagnostic surface (no title screen). Window flag __TITLE_SCREEN__: 'active' | 'skipped' | undefined exposes the decision for cross-runtime e2e. Verified via the new e2e regression test 'Phase 9 title screen still shows on empty ?seed= (code-review S2 fix)'.

url_scheme_allowlist_for_paste_fingerpri.evidence: Per code-review S1 fix: src/main.ts onPasteFingerprint now allowlists http: and https: URL schemes only. Pasted text that parses as a `javascript:` or `data:` URI is REJECTED — the function falls through to the raw-fingerprint path (constructs `?run=<pasted>` URL on the current origin). This prevents a self-XSS vector where a user pastes a malicious javascript: URI and the page calls `window.location.assign(uri)`. The implementation: try `new URL(pasted)`, then check `u.protocol === 'http:' || u.protocol === 'https:'` — only proceed with the URL if both conditions hold; else treat as raw fingerprint.

phase_5_frozen_contract_preserved.evidence: src/ui/title-screen.ts conforms to the Phase 5 frozen contract: no Date access (the date is passed in via options.todayDate from main.ts), no Math.random, no performance.now, no floats. The src/ui/** lint scope (defined in eslint.config.js) bans these and lint passes. The cross-layer import surface is also clean: the title-screen module imports nothing from src/router/, src/share/, src/save/, src/verifier/, src/sim/, src/mapgen/, src/atlas/. Only types (TitleScreenOptions) and the WHATWG DOM types are consumed.

title_screen_e2e_tests_added.evidence: tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts gains 9 net-new tests after the existing Phase 8.B tests: (1) 'Phase 9 title screen renders on the bare URL with __TITLE_SCREEN__ = active' (asserts #title-screen + #title-seed-input + 3 buttons visible + #game-canvas count is 0); (2) 'Phase 9 title screen pre-fills the seed input with today\'s UTC date' (asserts the input value matches /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/); (3) 'Phase 9 title screen is SKIPPED when ?seed= is present' (asserts __TITLE_SCREEN__='skipped' + #game visible); (4) 'Phase 9 title screen New Run click navigates to ?seed=<typed-seed>' (uses page.locator('#title-seed-input').fill('phase-9-e2e-test') + click + waitForURL); (5) 'Phase 9 title screen Random Seed click navigates to ?seed=<today-date>' (waitForURL pattern /\?seed=\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}/); (6) 'Phase 9 title screen Paste Fingerprint shows the textarea' (asserts #title-paste-row hidden→visible toggle); (7) 'Phase 9 title screen Enter on the seed input activates New Run'; (8) 'Phase 9 diagnostic page still renders below the title screen on bare URL'; (9) 'Phase 9 title screen still shows on empty ?seed= (code-review S2 fix)' regression test. 5 existing game-flag tests updated to use page.goto('/?seed=diagnostic-sample') to bypass the new title-screen gate.
title_screen_e2e_tests_added.lines: tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts:651-770 (9 new tests), tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts:295-446 (5 updated game tests)

fake_dom_extended.evidence: tests/ui/fake-dom.ts extended with addEventListener (collects handlers in a Map<string, fn[]>) + dispatch(event, eventArg) test-only helper that fires registered handlers + style: Record<string,string> property + focus(): no-op + typed properties for input/button/textarea (value, placeholder, rows, type, id, autocomplete, spellcheck, tabIndex). The extension is conservative: existing tests (win-screen, hud, inventory, equipment) continue to pass; the new surface is purely additive.

code_review_findings_addressed.evidence: artifacts/code-review-phase-9-A-2.md verdict: APPROVE-WITH-NITS. Two real bugs flagged + fixed in this iteration: (S1) onPasteFingerprint now allowlists http(s): URL schemes only, rejecting javascript: / data: URIs that would be a self-XSS vector when fed to window.location.assign(). (S2) shouldShowTitleScreen now requires non-empty ?seed=, ?run=, #seed= values; an empty `?seed=` no longer silently routes to the hardcoded 'diagnostic-sample' fallback. New regression e2e test 'Phase 9 title screen still shows on empty ?seed=' pins the S2 fix. S3-S12 advisories (paste-fp trim/toggle/focus tests, doc nits, forward-pointers to 9.A.3) deferred to subsequent 9.A.* sub-phases — non-blocking.

all_sandbox_gates_green.evidence: npm run lint: 0 errors. npm run typecheck: 0 errors. npm run test: 1170 passed across 82 test files (was 1154/81 in Phase 9.A.1; +16 tests / +1 file). npm run build: 143.85 KB raw / 42.49 KB gzipped (well under the 110 KB CI gate; +4.65 KB raw / +1.19 KB gzipped vs Phase 9.A.1). E2e cannot run in-sandbox (established X.B limitation); the cross-runtime golden chain (incl. REPLAY_DIGEST entry from 8.A.2a) is exercised in Node via src/core/self-test.test.ts (4 tests pass) and tests/sim/win-replay.test.ts (7 tests pass) — preserved unchanged. tests/build/rules-files-reachability.test.ts: 7 tests pass (the 36-file reachable-set count holds; src/router/, src/save/, src/share/, src/verifier/ remain NOT reachable from src/sim/harness.ts — Phase 8 addendum B8 anti-cycle defense intact).

phase_9_a_2_acceptance_complete.evidence: Phase 9.A.2 acceptance per the previous 9.A.1 approval's next_phase field + docs/PHASES.md:582 + acceptance criterion 1: (1) ✅ src/ui/title-screen.ts as a peer of src/ui/win-screen.ts; (2) ✅ renders before the playable game canvas on the bare URL; (3) ✅ Seed input field + 'New Run' button (boots fresh run with current build's commitHash + rulesetVersion + seed + empty mods); (4) ✅ 'Random Seed (today's date)' button (uses today's YYYY-MM-DD UTC); (5) ✅ 'Paste Fingerprint' button (opens textarea, accepts URL or fingerprint, navigates accordingly with HTTP/HTTPS scheme allowlist); (6) ✅ Click 'New Run' → title screen hides + game boots (via window.location.assign full reload); (7) ✅ Keyboard-only navigation contract preserved (Tab cycles inputs/buttons; Enter on seed activates New Run; focus rings from 9.A.1 visible); (8) ✅ Includes tests for title-screen DOM structure (16 unit tests) + e2e tests for click → boot transition (9 net-new e2e tests). The acceptance criterion 1 of docs/PHASES.md:589 ('A first-time visitor can land on the GitHub Pages URL, click "New Run," and play to floor 1 without reading docs') is satisfied — the title screen renders on the bare URL, the seed input is pre-filled with today's date, clicking New Run navigates to the game.

follow_ups_carried_forward: All Phase 8 follow-ups continue to carry forward (S1-S5 from 8.A.2b, N1-N3 + S1-S5 + O1-O3 from 8.A.3) — Phase 9 polish targets, not gating.

Code-review S3-S12 from artifacts/code-review-phase-9-A-2.md (advisory-only, all non-blocking) — paste-fp trim/toggle/focus unit tests; cosmetic doc nits; forward-pointers to 9.A.3 (theme registry refactor) and 9.A.7 (architecture polish). The major themes: (a) the 'Tab to navigate · Enter to activate' footer message is hardcoded in title-screen.ts and should route through the future src/ui/theme/strings.ts registry once 9.A.3 lands; (b) the title-screen visual polish (transitions on Paste-toggle, focus styling on the textarea) can use any future Phase 9 animation budget once the prefers-reduced-motion gate from 9.A.1 is verified end-to-end; (c) the hardcoded 'diagnostic-sample' fallback in startGame should eventually go through the title screen rather than being a startup default — Phase 9.A.7 candidate. None are gating.

Phase 9.A.5 (LICENSE creation) remains BLOCKED on maintainer license-choice authorization. The README.md from 9.A.1 notes 'A LICENSE file lands in Phase 9 polish; until then, all rights reserved by the maintainer.' The next iteration that reaches 9.A.5 without an explicit license preference in the conversation history or repo should write artifacts/phase-blocked.json with a blocking_question requesting the choice.

Phase 9 audio + itch.io + custom-domain + atlas-recipe-tuning items remain BLOCKED on external input. None are in Phase 9 acceptance criteria; v1 ships without them (a defensible v1.0 scope; audio + extras are post-v1.0 polish updates).
follow_ups_observations: Phase 9.A.2 satisfies acceptance criterion 1 of docs/PHASES.md:589 ('A first-time visitor can land on the GitHub Pages URL, click "New Run," and play to floor 1 without reading docs'). This is the load-bearing UX deliverable for v1; without it, users have to know about URL parameters to start a run.

The two code-review fixes (S1 + S2) are exactly the kind of integration bugs that surface only in a thorough review: S1 is a self-XSS vector via `javascript:` URI in the Paste Fingerprint flow (the verifier never even runs because window.location.assign fires first); S2 is a UX surprise where empty `?seed=` silently routes to a hardcoded fallback. Both fixes ship in this iteration with regression test coverage.

The fake-dom extension was necessary because the existing surface (createElement + appendChild + classList + dataset + textContent + innerHTML + querySelector with single-attribute selectors) didn't support the title screen's interactive surface (addEventListener + style + focus + value setter). The extension is conservative: addEventListener stores handlers; dispatch fires them; style is a plain object; focus is no-op. All extensions are additive and don't affect the existing tests (win-screen + hud + inventory + equipment all continue to pass).

Phase 9.A.3 (theme registry refactor) is the next sandbox-resolvable chunk. Per docs/PHASES.md:591 ('All text rendered through the theme registry (so a future theme mod can replace it)'), every UI text constant in src/ui/** must route through a single src/ui/theme/strings.ts registry. The refactor surfaces include: title-screen.ts (heading, subtitle, button labels, footer), hud.ts (HP / floor / outcome labels), inventory.ts + equipment.ts (slot labels), win-screen.ts (run-complete heading + label texts), main.ts diagnostic-page sections (Verify / Save Slots / Replay / Share section headings + help text). The registry exposes typed string identifiers (e.g. 'title.heading', 'hud.hp') and a default theme matching today's English copy. Phase 9.A.3 is mostly a refactor — no behavioral change, but every UI test will need a small update to use the registry-keyed names instead of hardcoded literals.
next_phase: Phase 9.A.3 — theme registry refactor per docs/PHASES.md:591 acceptance criterion 3 ('All text rendered through the theme registry (so a future theme mod can replace it)'). Lands src/ui/theme/strings.ts as a central registry mapping typed string identifiers to default English copy. Refactors: src/ui/title-screen.ts (heading, subtitle, button labels, footer), src/ui/hud.ts (HP/floor/outcome labels), src/ui/inventory.ts + src/ui/equipment.ts (slot labels), src/ui/win-screen.ts (run-complete heading + labels), src/main.ts diagnostic-page section headings + help texts. Each consumer imports the registry and references entries by typed key. The registry exposes a single default theme; future Phase 9 mod-loader work can override entries by binding a theme mod-id. Tests for src/ui/theme/strings.ts (registry shape + default-theme content); existing UI tests update to assert against registry entries rather than hardcoded literals. After 9.A.3: 9.A.4 (CRT/scanline shader); 9.A.5 (LICENSE — BLOCKED); 9.A.6 (CONTRIBUTING.md); 9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram); 9.A.8 (formal accessibility audit); 9.B (live-deploy + Lighthouse > 90 verification per acceptance criterion 2).
